verses.latin_vulgate: Melior est buccella sicca cum gaudio  quam domus plena victimis cum jurgio.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ט֤וֹב פַּ֣ת חֲ֭רֵבָה וְשַׁלְוָה־בָ֑הּ מִ֝בַּ֗יִת מָלֵ֥א זִבְחֵי־רִֽיב׃
verses.king_james: Better is a dry morsel, and quietness therewith, than an house full of sacrifices with strife.

verses.latin_vulgate: Servus sapiens dominabitur filiis stultis,  et inter fratres hæreditatem dividet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֶֽבֶד־מַשְׂכִּ֗יל יִ֭מְשֹׁל בְּבֵ֣ן מֵבִ֑ישׁ וּבְת֥וֹךְ אַ֝חִ֗ים יַחֲלֹ֥ק נַחֲלָֽה׃
verses.king_james: A wise servant shall have rule over a son that causeth shame, and shall have part of the inheritance among the brethren.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut igne probatur argentum et aurum camino,  ita corda probat Dominus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַצְרֵ֣ף לַ֭כֶּסֶף וְכ֣וּר לַזָּהָ֑ב וּבֹחֵ֖ן לִבּ֣וֹת יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.king_james: The fining pot is for silver, and the furnace for gold: but the Lord trieth the hearts.

verses.latin_vulgate: Malus obedit linguæ iniquæ,  et fallax obtemperat labiis mendacibus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מֵ֭רַע מַקְשִׁ֣יב עַל־שְׂפַת־אָ֑וֶן שֶׁ֥קֶר מֵ֝זִ֗ין עַל־לְשׁ֥וֹן הַוֺּֽת׃
verses.king_james: A wicked doer giveth heed to false lips; and a liar giveth ear to a naughty tongue.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui despicit pauperem exprobrat factori ejus,  et qui ruina lætatur alterius non erit impunitus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹעֵ֣ג לָ֭רָשׁ חֵרֵ֣ף עֹשֵׂ֑הוּ שָׂמֵ֥חַ לְ֝אֵ֗יד לֹ֣א יִנָּקֶֽה׃
verses.king_james: Whoso mocketh the poor reproacheth his Maker: and he that is glad at calamities shall not be unpunished.

verses.latin_vulgate: Corona senum filii filiorum,  et gloria filiorum patres eorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֲטֶ֣רֶת זְ֭קֵנִים בְּנֵ֣י בָנִ֑ים וְתִפְאֶ֖רֶת בָּנִ֣ים אֲבוֹתָֽם׃
verses.king_james: Children’s children are the crown of old men; and the glory of children are their fathers.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non decent stultum verba composita,  nec principem labium mentiens.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹא־נָאוָ֣ה לְנָבָ֣ל שְׂפַת־יֶ֑תֶר אַ֝֗ף כִּֽי־לְנָדִ֥יב שְׂפַת־שָֽׁקֶר׃
verses.king_james: Excellent speech becometh not a fool: much less do lying lips a prince.

verses.latin_vulgate: Gemma gratissima exspectatio præstolantis ;  quocumque se vertit, prudenter intelligit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֶבֶן־חֵ֣ן הַ֭שֹּׁחַד בְּעֵינֵ֣י בְעָלָ֑יו אֶֽל־כׇּל־אֲשֶׁ֖ר יִפְנֶ֣ה יַשְׂכִּֽיל׃
verses.king_james: A gift is as a precious stone in the eyes of him that hath it: whithersoever it turneth, it prospereth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui celat delictum quærit amicitias ;  qui altero sermone repetit, separat fœderatos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מְֽכַסֶּה־פֶּ֭שַׁע מְבַקֵּ֣שׁ אַהֲבָ֑ה וְשֹׁנֶ֥ה בְ֝דָבָ֗ר מַפְרִ֥יד אַלּֽוּף׃
verses.king_james: He that covereth a transgression seeketh love; but he that repeateth a matter separateth very friends.

verses.latin_vulgate: Plus proficit correptio apud prudentem,  quam centum plagæ apud stultum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תֵּ֣חַת גְּעָרָ֣ה בְמֵבִ֑ין מֵהַכּ֖וֹת כְּסִ֣יל מֵאָֽה׃
verses.king_james: A reproof entereth more into a wise man than an hundred stripes into a fool.

verses.latin_vulgate: Semper jurgia quærit malus :  angelus autem crudelis mittetur contra eum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַךְ־מְרִ֥י יְבַקֶּשׁ־רָ֑ע וּמַלְאָ֥ךְ אַ֝כְזָרִ֗י יְשֻׁלַּח־בּֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: An evil man seeketh only rebellion: therefore a cruel messenger shall be sent against him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Expedit magis ursæ occurrere raptis fœtibus,  quam fatuo confidenti in stultitia sua.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: פָּג֬וֹשׁ דֹּ֣ב שַׁכּ֣וּל בְּאִ֑ישׁ וְאַל־כְּ֝סִ֗יל בְּאִוַּלְתּֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Let a bear robbed of her whelps meet a man, rather than a fool in his folly.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui reddit mala pro bonis,  non recedet malum de domo ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מֵשִׁ֣יב רָ֭עָה תַּ֣חַת טוֹבָ֑ה לֹא־[תָמ֥וּשׁ] (תמיש) רָ֝עָ֗ה מִבֵּיתֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Whoso rewardeth evil for good, evil shall not depart from his house.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui dimittit aquam caput est jurgiorum,  et antequam patiatur contumeliam judicium deserit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: פּ֣וֹטֵֽר מַ֭יִם רֵאשִׁ֣ית מָד֑וֹן וְלִפְנֵ֥י הִ֝תְגַּלַּ֗ע הָרִ֥יב נְטֽוֹשׁ׃
verses.king_james: The beginning of strife is as when one letteth out water: therefore leave off contention, before it be meddled with.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui justificat impium, et qui condemnat justum,  abominabilis est uterque apud Deum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַצְדִּ֣יק רָ֭שָׁע וּמַרְשִׁ֣יעַ צַדִּ֑יק תּוֹעֲבַ֥ת יְ֝הֹוָ֗ה גַּם־שְׁנֵיהֶֽם׃
verses.king_james: He that justifieth the wicked, and he that condemneth the just, even they both are abomination to the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid prodest stulto habere divitias,  cum sapientiam emere non possit ?  Qui altum facit domum suam quærit ruinam,  et qui evitat discere incidet in mala.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָמָּה־זֶּ֣ה מְחִ֣יר בְּיַד־כְּסִ֑יל לִקְנ֖וֹת חׇכְמָ֣ה וְלֶב־אָֽיִן׃
verses.king_james: Wherefore is there a price in the hand of a fool to get wisdom, seeing he hath no heart to it?

verses.latin_vulgate: Omni tempore diligit qui amicus est,  et frater in angustiis comprobatur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּכׇל־עֵ֭ת אֹהֵ֣ב הָרֵ֑עַ וְאָ֥ח לְ֝צָרָ֗ה יִוָּלֵֽד׃
verses.king_james: A friend loveth at all times, and a brother is born for adversity.

verses.latin_vulgate: Stultus homo plaudet manibus,  cum spoponderit pro amico suo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָדָ֣ם חֲסַר־לֵ֭ב תּוֹקֵ֣עַ כָּ֑ף עֹרֵ֥ב עֲ֝רֻבָּ֗ה לִפְנֵ֥י רֵעֵֽהוּ׃
verses.king_james: A man void of understanding striketh hands, and becometh surety in the presence of his friend.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui meditatur discordias diligit rixas,  et qui exaltat ostium quærit ruinam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֹ֣הֵֽב פֶּ֭שַׁע אֹהֵ֣ב מַצָּ֑ה מַגְבִּ֥יהַּ פִּ֝תְח֗וֹ מְבַקֶּשׁ־שָֽׁבֶר׃
verses.king_james: He loveth transgression that loveth strife: and he that exalteth his gate seeketh destruction.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui perversi cordis est non inveniet bonum,  et qui vertit linguam incidet in malum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עִקֶּשׁ־לֵ֭ב לֹ֣א יִמְצָא־ט֑וֹב וְנֶהְפָּ֥ךְ בִּ֝לְשׁוֹנ֗וֹ יִפּ֥וֹל בְּרָעָֽה׃
verses.king_james: He that hath a froward heart findeth no good: and he that hath a perverse tongue falleth into mischief.

verses.latin_vulgate: Natus est stultus in ignominiam suam ;  sed nec pater in fatuo lætabitur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יֹלֵ֣ד כְּ֭סִיל לְת֣וּגָה ל֑וֹ וְלֹא־יִ֝שְׂמַ֗ח אֲבִ֣י נָבָֽל׃
verses.king_james: He that begetteth a fool doeth it to his sorrow: and the father of a fool hath no joy.

verses.latin_vulgate: Animus gaudens ætatem floridam facit ;  spiritus tristis exsiccat ossa.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֵ֣ב שָׂ֭מֵחַ יֵיטִ֣יב גֵּהָ֑ה וְר֥וּחַ נְ֝כֵאָ֗ה תְּיַבֶּשׁ־גָּֽרֶם׃
verses.king_james: A merry heart doeth good like a medicine: but a broken spirit drieth the bones.

verses.latin_vulgate: Munera de sinu impius accipit,  ut pervertat semitas judicii.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שֹׁ֣חַד מֵ֭חֵק רָשָׁ֣ע יִקָּ֑ח לְ֝הַטּ֗וֹת אׇרְח֥וֹת מִשְׁפָּֽט׃
verses.king_james: A wicked man taketh a gift out of the bosom to pervert the ways of judgment.

verses.latin_vulgate: In facie prudentis lucet sapientia ;  oculi stultorum in finibus terræ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֶת־פְּנֵ֣י מֵבִ֣ין חׇכְמָ֑ה וְעֵינֵ֥י כְ֝סִ֗יל בִּקְצֵה־אָֽרֶץ׃
verses.king_james: Wisdom is before him that hath understanding; but the eyes of a fool are in the ends of the earth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ira patris filius stultus,  et dolor matris quæ genuit eum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כַּ֣עַס לְ֭אָבִיו בֵּ֣ן כְּסִ֑יל וּ֝מֶ֗מֶר לְיֽוֹלַדְתּֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: A foolish son is a grief to his father, and bitterness to her that bare him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non est bonum damnum inferre justo,  nec percutere principem qui recta judicat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּ֤ם עֲנ֣וֹשׁ לַצַּדִּ֣יק לֹא־ט֑וֹב לְהַכּ֖וֹת נְדִיבִ֣ים עַל־יֹֽשֶׁר׃
verses.king_james: Also to punish the just is not good, nor to strike princes for equity.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui moderatur sermones suos doctus et prudens est,  et pretiosi spiritus vir eruditus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חוֹשֵׂ֣ךְ אֲ֭מָרָיו יוֹדֵ֣עַ דָּ֑עַת (וקר) [יְקַר־]ר֝֗וּחַ אִ֣ישׁ תְּבוּנָֽה׃
verses.king_james: He that hath knowledge spareth his words: and a man of understanding is of an excellent spirit.

verses.latin_vulgate: Stultus quoque, si tacuerit, sapiens reputabitur,  et si compresserit labia sua, intelligens.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּ֤ם אֱוִ֣יל מַ֭חֲרִישׁ חָכָ֣ם יֵחָשֵׁ֑ב אֹטֵ֖ם שְׂפָתָ֣יו נָבֽוֹן׃
verses.king_james: Even a fool, when he holdeth his peace, is counted wise: and he that shutteth his lips is esteemed a man of understanding.
